symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden pleuritic chest pain (worse on inspiration and coughing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pleuritic pain from pulmonary infarction is a common presenting feature of PE, though non-specific.
Pleuritic pain can be infective, but here without fever or sputum.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unilateral right calf swelling, warmth and tenderness after long-haul flight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinical DVT identifies the embolic source and is a heavily weighted Wells criterion, sharply raising PE probability.
A concurrent DVT redirects the whole picture toward VTE and away from a benign musculoskeletal cause.
Syncope/presyncope in PE marks haemodynamic compromise and predicts a massive/submassive embolus with high mortality.
Tachycardia is a Wells criterion and reflects cardiopulmonary compensation for embolic obstruction; patient-reported via wearable, unverified.
Rapid shallow breathing is typical of the ventilation-perfusion mismatch of PE.
Hypoxaemia out of proportion to lung findings supports a perfusion (vascular) cause; patient-obtainable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.
Prolonged immobility (Virchow's triad — stasis) is a major provoking VTE risk factor and a Wells criterion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever and purulent sputum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of fever and purulent sputum makes an infective pneumonia less likely than a vascular occlusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable right ventricular heave and loud P2 (acute right heart strain)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective signs of acute cor pulmonale — sealed clinician examination finding, not elicitable via telehealth; the AI Doctor infers strain from presyncope and hypoxaemia instead.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear lung fields on auscultation with disproportionate hypoxaemia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypoxaemia with a clear chest points to a vascular rather than parenchymal cause — a sealed auscultation finding.
Absence of crackles/consolidation argues against pneumonia.

symptoms.symptom_name: CT pulmonary angiography showing intraluminal filling defects

investigation_details.expected_result: Focal intraluminal filling defects in the pulmonary arteries confirming embolus
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: PE remains unconfirmed and untreated; definitive diagnosis is missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Filling defects on CTPA are the confirmatory gold-standard — a hospital investigation, never available via telehealth.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: At high pre-test probability D-dimer does not change management — imaging is required regardless; a negative result must not be used to exclude PE here.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sensitive but non-specific; useful only to exclude PE at LOW pre-test probability, not at the high probability seen here.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Pleuritic chest pain + unilateral calf DVT + tachycardia/tachypnoea + recent long-haul immobility together constitute a high-probability VTE emergency pattern.
Presyncope, hypoxaemia and right heart strain together signal a haemodynamically significant embolus at risk of sudden deterioration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive confirmation of PE; the reason for immediate emergency transport — cannot be performed in telehealth.
Exclude acute coronary syndrome and assess right heart strain; part of the emergency workup, not obtainable remotely.
At high Wells pre-test probability a D-dimer must NOT be used to exclude PE and must not delay imaging — over-reliance on it here is an error.
